how_verified: BY USE, chat-4b resident (company gpu: chat-4b ~8.0GB). (a) UNIT over mock verdict-sets: all-pass->green, one-fail->fail, one-uncertain->flag; plus the vacuous-green guard (empty set->flag, NOT green: all([])==True trap closed), fail-dominance (fail beats uncertain), and missing/unknown/malformed verdict->flag (never silently green). (a') the SAME tally over a REAL store: 3 mock verify_lens outputs persisted (put_content->set_ref) -> run_reduce(mode='rule', reduce_rule='verdict-tally') -> fail; read-back value confirmed verdict is a TOP-LEVEL field (shape verified, not inferred). (b) FULL LIVE CHAIN via the MCP tools: run_items('verify_lens', [{lens:correctness},{lens:adversarial-disprove}] over a buggy empty-list-unguarded fn) -> both 4B jurors returned verdict=fail with breaking_case 'values=[]' -> run_reduce(rule, verdict-tally) -> {tally:'fail', n:2, verdicts:['fail','fail']}. (c) reduce_rule_names() now returns ['concat','count','first','verdict-tally'] AND == sorted(_REDUCE_RULES) (derives from the single source; the run_reduce description + fail-loud error also derive). (d) FLOOR: mcp_engine_acceptance 35/35 PASS, cognition_governance_acceptance 42/42 PASS (the AST source-invariant scans mcp_face/server.py; _REDUCE_RULES is pure computation, no resolve/approve/dispatch, no claude -p), reduce_acceptance 28/28, cascade_acceptance 25/25, drift_acceptance 5/5. (2) save_cascade('verify-jury') verified in a TEMP store first, then SAVED CANONICALLY to the live store (.data/store/cascades.json — confirmed persisted); decl = [{op:generate, role:verify_lens, kind:items} -> {op:reduce, role:verify_lens, reduce_mode:rule, reduce_rule:verdict-tally}], output_schema {tally:str, n:int, verdicts:list[str]}. The reduce step declares role:'verify_lens' because cognition.run_cascade (out of lane) REQUIRES every step to name a registered role (cognition.py:2034) and uses role.id only as the reduce-output address label (cognition.py:2077) — in rule mode the model never fires (run_reduce gets only reduce_rule, not role). save_cascade succeeds because build_action validates only op+model, not reduce_rule names.
needs_tim: run_cascade('verify-jury') END-TO-END is BLOCKED by a cross-lane gap (VERIFIED, not inferred): the cascade path uses a SEPARATE rule registry runtime/suite.py:842 _CASCADE_REDUCE_RULES (passed to cognition.run_cascade as reduce_rules) which is a PARALLEL LITERAL to mcp_face/server.py:_REDUCE_RULES (the suite comment even says 'mirrors the MCP run_reduce _REDUCE_RULES seam' — a built-twice/drift hazard). VERIFIED that the cross-lane dict is the ONLY blocker (not G3): with the rule injected in-memory (a verification fixture, no file edit — mirrors the wire's injectable committer) run_cascade('verify-jury', inputs=[2 lens-units]) ran END-TO-END -> step 0 FANNED the inputs list as N=2 units (two addresses run://.../verify_lens/0 and /1, confirming step-0 list-fan works in the cascade runner — NOT blocked on G3 multi-var substitution), step 1 tallied -> final_output {tally:'fail', n:2, verdicts:['fail','fail']}. So the saved cascade is genuinely re-runnable the moment verdict-tally is added to suite._CASCADE_REDUCE_RULES. NOT green-painted: the deliverable's by-use (a/b/c/d) never call run_cascade; the direct run_items->run_reduce(rule) chain (b) is proven; the canonical run_cascade('verify-jury') stays fail-loud until the one-line cross-lane dict-add.
notes: G9 closes the verify-jury per SYSTEM-GAPS G9 + COMPOSITIONS ⑥ (verification jury). verify_lens (roles/verify_lens.py) was already built+live; this lane adds the DETERMINISTIC verdict-TALLY reduce-rule that completes it. The rule is the floor (pure (values)->dict, no model). It collects each output's 'verdict' field (the closed pass|fail|uncertain vocab VerifyLensOut emits) and returns {tally:'green'|'fail'|'flag', n, verdicts}: 'fail' if ANY=='fail'; 'green' iff NON-EMPTY and every=='pass'; else 'flag'. The non-empty guard is the key deviation from the literal spec ('green iff every=='pass') because all([])==True would make a zero-juror set green (the exact false-pass a verification jury exists to prevent) -> empty/missing/unknown all route to 'flag'. Mirrors the existing count/concat/first lambda shape exactly; lives in the single-source _REDUCE_RULES so reduce_rule_names()/description/error all derive. Files touched: ONLY mcp_face/server.py (the rule + nothing else) + the canonical save_cascade write to the live cascades.json. No commit (per instructions). Self-description: drift_acceptance passes (a reduce-rule is surfaced via reduce_rule_names(), not a MAP-registry capability block, so no forced STATE/MAP edit). Did NOT touch cognition.py/suite.py/roles.py/roles/AGENTS.md/the verify_lens role.
